top_signals.title: Spyware found on phone of European Parliament member probing it
top_signals.summary: Stelios Kouloglou, formerly a member of the European Parliament's committee investigating abuses of commercial spyware, was twice infected with Pegasus while serving, researchers said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Colombia's Oil and Gas Reserves Keep Shrinking
top_signals.summary: For a decade, Colombia's economically vital oil industry has been caught in a death spiral. A combination of sharply weaker oil prices, rising geopolitical risk and anti-petroleum industry reforms implemented by Colombia's first leftist president Gustavo Petro deterred investment, causing the operational tempo to decline. The main issue weighing heavily on the oil patch's outlook is a dire lack of proven petroleum reserves. These are the result of a lack of spending on wildcat drilling and a poor exploration success rate. After a lengthy delay,…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Bitcoin profit and loss ratio falls to 43-month low
top_signals.summary: Bitwise chief investment officer Matt Hougan said the bottom is “closer than ever,” while a Swan Bitcoin analyst suggested investors buy now at a discount rather than overpaying later.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: FSCS Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements 2025/26
top_signals.summary: In its Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements published today (Thursday 2 July, 2026), the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) outlines the vital work it has carried out during the past financial year.

top_signals.title: US law enforcement group drops opposition to CLARITY Act: Report
top_signals.summary: The Major County Sheriffs of America said it still wants the CLARITY Act amended to give local law enforcement more resources to investigate illicit finance cases.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Industrial sector still lags on Asian representation, says Lee
Worse Than an Axis
top_signals.summary: The informal alignment of U.S. adversaries is dangerous.

top_signals.title: Europe Goes Its Own Way
top_signals.summary: Drifting from America, the continent Is rearming and reordering itself.

top_signals.title: Supreme Court decision threatens EU-US data transfer agreement
top_signals.summary: In a Tuesday letter, Max Schrems, the founder of the Vienna-based privacy advocacy organization noyb, told European officials he plans to sue to invalidate the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) that allows for the transfer of personal data from the EU to U.S. companies.

alerts.title: Supreme Court decision threatens EU-US data transfer agreement
alerts.summary: In a Tuesday letter, Max Schrems, the founder of the Vienna-based privacy advocacy organization noyb, told European officials he plans to sue to invalidate the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) that allows for the transfer of personal data from the EU to U.S. companies.
